title: UAV Systems Engineer - Applied Autonomy Startup
setting: A venture-backed UAV company building mission-specific aircraft for inspection, mapping, or public-safety work
companySize: Systems team of 6-15 inside a startup of 30-80 people
description: You are the connective tissue between the aircraft, payload, autonomy stack, and customer mission. The role is less about owning one subsystem and more about making the whole system behave coherently in the real world.

schedule.activity: Review mission logs and open integration issues from the previous day's field test
schedule.note: Systems work starts with yesterday's mismatches between expected and actual behaviour.

schedule.activity: Run a system stand-up across airframe, software, payload, and operations leads
schedule.note: Most blockers live at the interface between teams.

schedule.activity: Update requirements, interfaces, and acceptance criteria for the next build
schedule.note: The goal is to turn field lessons into better system definition.

schedule.activity: Support a simulation or bench test of communications and mission logic
schedule.note: You are checking system behaviour, not only component function.

schedule.activity: Draft a revised CONOPS for a customer use case or operational constraint
schedule.note: Operations often change the vehicle faster than the other way around.

schedule.activity: Prepare the next test objective and close risk items with the team
schedule.note: The day ends by choosing what to learn next, not by claiming everything is done.

responsibilities: Translate mission needs into system requirements
Coordinate subsystem interfaces and acceptance criteria
Support simulation, bench, and field integration
Help turn test results into system decisions
challenges: Constant trade-offs between mission ambition, autonomy, and regulatory reality
Rapid change across multiple interacting subsystems
Operating with incomplete data while the product is still evolving
rewards: Seeing the full system, not just one narrow discipline
Working close to both engineering and customer mission outcomes
Learning faster than you would in a more siloed environment
careerPath: Systems analyst -> UAV systems engineer -> Senior systems engineer -> Program systems lead -> Head of systems engineering
